title: potentilla thuringiaca f. humilior
intro: Potentilla thuringiaca f. humilior, also known as the dwarf potentilla, is a low-growing, deciduous shrub native to Europe and Asia, belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties, which are attributed to its tannins and flavonoids content. The health benefits of Potentilla thuringiaca f. humilior include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant activity, making it useful for treating various skin conditions, such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and topical creams, which can be used to support skin health and reduce inflammation.
